step_by_step_thinking: I largely concur that Henoch-Schönlein Purpura (HSP) is highly probable based on both the clinical presentation and biopsy findings indicating perivascular IgA deposits. The key distinguishing feature here is the precise match between the patient’s symptoms—specifically non-bullating macules and pappules—and what we know about typical presentations of HSP. While there are indeed numerous dermato-logical conditions capable of mimicking some aspects of HSP, no other listed option perfectly corresponds to all elements observed in our case. Therefore, I maintain my agreement with selecting A. ## Your Response:
